title: Empty SQL result (no rows, no columns)
description: Execution of a valid SQL SELECT-query produces a completely empty result - there are no even columns.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test1 (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(100), created_at TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test1 (id, name, created_at) VALUES (1, 'Alice', TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 10:00:00');
CREATE TABLE test2 (value INTEGER, description LONG VARCHAR, active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE);
INSERT INTO test2 (value, description, active) VALUES (NULL, 'Test entry', NULL);
SELECT (SELECT 1) AS dummy_col FROM test1 WHERE id = 2 LIMIT 1;
key_bug_features: Valid SQL SELECT-query returns empty result with no columns
